goal: Evaluate a purpose-built Single Layer Water material on the custom C++ solver-owned procedural river ribbon without requiring WaterBody or WaterZone authority.

test_run.build: SmokeEmIfYouGotEmEditor Mac Development succeeded with UE_5.8

observed_failure.summary: The engine WaterAdvanced material candidate produced a near-black river surface on the generated procedural river ribbon, making the downstream river-eye captures worse than the active vertex-color water preview.
likely_causes: plugin water material expects WaterBody or WaterZone data not present on the generated ProceduralMeshComponent ribbon
default plugin material parameters are not tuned for the custom preview mesh, manual exposure, or source-mask-driven river colors
the generated mesh currently needs a purpose-built material or Water plugin integration rather than a direct material swap
blocked_use: do not use the tested engine water material candidate as the active generated preview water path
do not claim Water plugin integration is validated for the preview river ribbon
do not overwrite the currently readable vertex-color water captures with the failed black-water captures

active_decision.decision: Keep the DefaultLit parent on legacy/base preview maps while the isolated Landscape candidates evaluate the custom Single Layer Water parent and three river-specific material instances.
active_decision.single_layer_water_contract: Opaque SingleLayerWater shading uses river-specific scattering, absorption, phase g, behind-water color scale, two-scale first-party normal-atlas sampling, bounded surface/vertex tint, roughness, specular, refraction IOR, and a bounded Fresnel sky-fill fallback. South Fork additionally samples clamped macro normals and packed depth/speed/Froude generated from its accepted whole-window median finite-volume C++ frame. A movable skylight and runtime corridor reflection capture are present; offscreen evidence capture temporarily forces the reflection-cubemap technique without changing saved game settings.
active_decision.geometry_authority: The custom C++ water simulation and generated ribbon geometry remain authoritative. The solver visualization derivative is material-only, feature forcing remains disabled, and neither it nor render-normal smoothing, render-only width overlap, or Fresnel fallback can change solver state, collision, raft forces, or conservation evidence.
active_decision.observed_result: All three material instances compile and bind to the generated procedural ribbons with clean map checks and nonblack captures. South Fork now carries broad macro-normal, depth, speed, and Froude variation from a GeoClaw-compared C++ frame; Colorado and Pacuare explicitly do not reuse those fields. The low-resolution derivative adds physically grounded variation but does not make the synthetic ribbon lifelike because riverbed/bank geometry, live displacement, foam, spray, atmosphere, and biome assets are not production quality.
active_decision.promotion_gate: Do not promote the candidate until live-editor and packaged captures show credible reflection/refraction, solver-aligned flow detail, bank/riverbed transitions, foam and spray, hazard and swimmer/rescue readability, guide review, and measured desktop/VR performance for every seasonal flow band.

next_actions: Extend the static South Fork review derivative to live per-frame solver displacement, velocity-aligned micro-normal advection, wet/dry transitions, and aeration masks while keeping feature forcing disabled by default.
Generate Colorado and Pacuare fields only from their own accepted C++/GeoClaw scenarios; never reuse South Fork hydraulic textures across rivers.
Build production riverbed and wet-bank material transitions so shallow refraction does not expose coarse Landscape channel-burn geometry.
Add solver-aligned foam, spray, mist, and whitewater VFX for holes, wave trains, laterals, eddy lines, shelves, and boulder interactions.
Capture the same material in the live editor and packaged game because commandlet SceneCapture reflection behavior is not sufficient evidence for promotion.
Keep regenerated captures honest: this is a review candidate, not lifelike or production-approved water.
